chapter_question: How does the Gauss map convert changing normals into signed area distortion?
inspection_targets: A surface patch and its normals reveal signed area change.
The normal image on the sphere is the curvature measurement.
Elliptic, hyperbolic, parabolic, and planar points are separated.
Product and average of principal curvatures answer different questions.

source_span.focus: Defines extrinsic curvature through the spherical map and explores the local shapes permitted by principal curvature signs.
source_span.label: Chapter 12
source_span.title: The Extrinsic Curvature of a Surface
topics: Area distortion under the spherical map
Extrinsic curvature as product of principal curvatures
Elliptic, hyperbolic, parabolic, and planar points
Relation between sign of curvature and surface bending
